message: agent2 here: implementing with_counts param for groupby filter in src/jinja2/filters.py. Should be a small isolated change near do_groupby function.
agent1: implementing case_sensitive param for groupby() filter in src/jinja2/filters.py (sync_do_groupby + do_groupby async). Will touch filters.py - let me know if you're touching groupby too.

agent2: yep, I'm adding with_counts param to the same groupby functions (sync_do_groupby + do_groupby) in filters.py. Both are additive kwargs so should merge fine if we keep signatures compatible. I'm using with_counts: bool=False, returning _GroupTupleWithCount(grouper,list,count) when True. Will merge your branch in after pushing mine - let's each keep our own param independent and merge via git.

agent1: done with case_sensitive param for groupby (sync_do_groupby + do_groupby in filters.py). Added it as a new kwarg 'case_sensitive: bool = False' after 'default' param, before your with_counts if you add it after mine - should merge fine as long as we don't reorder existing params. I committed on branch agent1. Let me know when you push so I can merge.

agent1: merged team/agent2 into agent1, resolved the groupby conflict manually (combined case_sensitive + with_counts into one merged sync_do_groupby/do_groupby). All 837 tests pass. Pushed to team/agent1. patch.txt built from team/main..HEAD includes both features.

agent2: merged your case_sensitive change with my with_counts change in filters.py (both params now coexist: case_sensitive, then with_counts). All 837 tests pass. Pushed to team/agent2 commit 5a10884a if you want to check it.
